extraction.one_line_summary: KUSS Sepp won stage 15 attacking on the final climb, while POGAČAR absorbed repeated attacks and retained yellow with comfortable control.
tactical_patterns: MATTHEWS Michael infiltrated early break despite sprint points available, showing GC teams comfortable with 25min+ deficit riders escaping.
POGAČAR Tadej positioned himself front of descent summit on final climb, revealing defensive priority over contesting stage win.
VAN AERT Wout sacrificed personal ambitions for KUSS alley-oop, demonstrating Jumbo's tactical flexibility to shift leader roles mid-stage.
WOODS Michael visible frustration at finish signals aggressive KOM jersey recapture attempt coming on next mountain stages.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: first Tour win since 2011 American drought, peaked perfectly on stage 15 after stage 11 win shows excellent grand tour stamina pattern
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: absorbed body-shot attacks without visible damage but team lost LAENGEN and two other crashers, thinning mountain support
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: won most aggressive despite switching domestique-to-leader roles, pocket-knife versatility makes him unpredictable tactical weapon
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: only sprinter motivated to chase 20-point intermediate, made 25-rider break suggesting underrated climbing when points justify effort
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: positive: moved to third overall as MARTIN Guillaume dropped, quietly banking GC position while others attack
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: seventh different polka dot wearer shows jersey vulnerability, visible frustration signals desperation mode for recapture
rider_signals.POELS Wout: neutral: seventh polka dot wearer by stage 15, early-race KOM form pattern repeating from Sky/Ineos days
